children.heading: Computation of student aid index
children.label: a Computation of student aid index
i
children.text: the family’s available income (determined in accordance with subsection (b)); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the family’s available assets (determined in accordance with subsection (c));

children.label: B
children.text: assessing such adjusted available income in accordance with an assessment schedule set forth in subsection (d); and

children.label: C
children.text: considering such assessment resulting under subparagraph (B) as the amount determined under this subsection.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: If the sum determined under paragraph (1) with respect to an independent student with dependents other than a spouse is less than − $1,500, the student aid index for the independent student shall be − $1,500.

children.label: A
children.text: Federal income taxes;

children.label: B
children.text: an allowance for payroll taxes, determined in accordance with paragraph (2);

children.label: C
children.text: an income protection allowance, determined in accordance with paragraph (3); and

children.label: D
children.text: an employment expense allowance, determined in accordance with paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: the total amount earned by the student (and spouse, if appropriate), multiplied by the rate of tax under ; andsection 3101(b) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: the amount earned by the student (and spouse, if appropriate) that does not exceed such contribution and benefit base (twice such contribution and benefit base, in the case of a joint return) for the year of the earnings, multiplied by the rate of tax applicable to such earnings under .section 3101(a) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: In the case of a married independent student with dependents:Income Protection Allowance (to be adjusted for 2023–2024 and succeeding years)Family Size (includingstudent)Amount3$46,1404$56,9705$67,2306$78,620For each additional add$8,880.

children.label: B
children.text: In the case of a single independent student with dependents:Income Protection Allowance (to be adjusted for 2023–2024 and succeeding years)Family Size (includingstudent)Amount2$43,9203$54,6904$67,5205$79,6806$93,180For each additional add$10,530.

children.heading: Employment expense allowance
children.label: 4 Employment expense allowance
children.text: The employment expense allowance is equal to the lesser of $4,000 or 35 percent of the student’s earned income or the combined earned income of the student and the student’s spouse, if applicable (as adjusted by the Secretary pursuant to ).section 1087rr(g) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the difference between the family’s assets (as defined in 1087vv(f) of this title) and the asset protection allowance (determined in accordance with paragraph (2)); multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: 7 percent.

children.heading: Not less than zero
children.label: B Not less than zero
children.text: The family’s available assets under this subsection shall not be less than zero.

children.heading: Assessment schedule
children.label: d Assessment schedule
children.text: The assessment of adjusted available income (as determined under subsection (a)(1) and hereafter in this subsection referred to as “AAI”) is calculated based on the following table (as revised by the Secretary pursuant to ):section 1087rr(e) of this titleAssessment From Adjusted Available IncomeIf AAI is—Then the assessment is—Less than − $6,820− $1,500− $6,820 to $17,40022% of AAI$17,401 to $21,800$3,828 + 25% of AAI over $17,400$21,801 to $26,200$4,928 + 29% of AAI over $21,800$26,201 to $30,700$6,204 + 34% of AAI over $26,200$30,701 to $35,100$7,734 + 40% of AAI over $30,700$35,101 or more$9,494 + 47% of AAI over $35,100.

children.heading: Computations in case of separation, divorce, or death
children.label: e Computations in case of separation, divorce, or death
children.text: In the case of a student who is divorced or separated, or whose spouse has died, the spouse’s income and assets shall not be considered in determining the family’s available income or assets.
